title: Spiced Egyptian Chicken Casserole
description: A comforting, aromatic casserole that blends tender chicken cooked sousvide with a fragrant blend of ginger, cumin, and coriander, finished in a slowroasted tomato sauce for a chewy, salty delight.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt, pepper, ground ginger, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika. Toss to coat evenly and let sit for 10 minutes. Heat 1 Tbsp of olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat and sear the thighs skin‑side down until golden, about 3 minutes; flip and sear the other side for 2 minutes. Transfer the seared thighs to a vacuum‑seal bag along with the remaining 1 Tbsp olive oil, garlic, tomato paste, broth, and lemon juice. Seal the bag and vacuum‑seal.

Place the sealed bag in a preheated sous‑vide water bath at 165 °F (74 °C). Cook for 90 minutes, then carefully remove the bag, discard the liquid, and pat the thighs dry. Arrange the thighs in a single layer in a 9‑inch baking dish and bake at 375 °F (190 °C) for 20 minutes, or until the skin is crisp and the sauce has thickened. Let rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with chopped cilantro if desired. Enjoy the chewy, savory layers of this Egyptian-inspired casserole.
